mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.01% on 2008-07-01 reflects Fed easing from prior tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month do not exceed baseline by at least 1.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.5% threshold, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, compressing near-term corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking equity prices adjust.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.7% on 2008-07-01 exceeds historical median
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly decline, indicating synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand. This interaction signal predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward in response to broad-based demand weakness.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1066.0K on 2008-06-01 reflects housing market stress
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline as specified, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 12-month moving average, signaling supply-side contraction and reduced capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward in response to production weakness.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.8419 on 2008-07-01 reflects manufacturing stress
failure_condition: When INDPRO < 12-month MA, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) move in opposite directions with PAYEMS declining and UNRATE rising simultaneously, creating an interaction signal of labor market deterioration. This joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices recession probability upward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137615.0K on 2008-07-01 shows employment weakness
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising unemployment
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises as specified, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while Federal funds rate remains below 3%, creating a real rate compression that signals monetary policy is accommodative. This regime-specific mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations expand under accommodative real rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 219.181 on 2008-07-01 reflects elevated inflation
regime_hint: accommodative real rates regime with inflation above target
failure_condition: When real rates are compressed as specified, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) declines below 2% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth and earnings multiples downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11700.6B on 2008-04-01 reflects slowing growth trajectory
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth is below 2% annualized, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 65% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines more than 1% month-over-month, signaling sharp supply-side shock that reduces near-term production capacity and corporate output. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity prices immediately reprice earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.8419 on 2008-07-01 reflects manufacturing sector stress
regime_hint: supply shock or production contraction phase
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 1% MoM, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.
